data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Shashank Samala that has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of six same-sector, same-stage private companies including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), alongside one public comparable. The peer set spans a wide range from $120M to $5.0B.

Two primary valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $150M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $285M. The peer-median method calculates the median valuation across the six same-sector, same-stage comparables, producing $355M.

These two approaches triangulate to a median valuation of $320M, with a supported range from $242M (low) to $408M (high). The 68% range reflects uncertainty around the appropriate peer multiple for Heirloom's specific technology maturity and market positioning relative to higher-outlier fusion systems companies versus lower-end carbon capture peers. The wide dispersion in comparable valuations—particularly Commonwealth Fusion's $5.0B relative to the $300–$400M cluster—introduces material uncertainty into any point estimate.
